seo.title: AI Bias Detection and Mitigation in DSPy Agents
seo.description: How to detect and fix ai bias detection and mitigation in DSPy agents. Framework-specific detection, fixes, and Vex automation.

content.intro: In DSPy agents, AI bias manifests through optimized prompts that overfit to biased training examples, producing skewed outputs across demographics. Chain-of-thought modules can amplify subtle biases into plausible but discriminatory reasoning paths, while module compositions unexpectedly shift output distributions toward unfair stereotypes. These issues evade traditional prompting safeguards since DSPy optimizes entire programs rather than individual prompts.

howItManifests.scenario: Optimized ChainOfThought module overfitting to gender-biased training data
howItManifests.symptom: Higher hiring recommendation scores for male-sounding names in resume evaluation
howItManifests.impact: Deployment leads to 25% gender disparity in candidate shortlisting

howItManifests.scenario: Signature module composition creating racial bias in sentiment analysis
howItManifests.symptom: Same neutral text receives 15% more negative sentiment when attributed to minority names
howItManifests.impact: Customer service escalation unfairly targets diverse customer segments

howItManifests.scenario: Retrieve + Generate pipeline amplifying geographic bias
howItManifests.symptom: Job recommendations favor urban candidates over rural ones with equal qualifications
howItManifests.impact: Regional hiring imbalances persist despite merit-based optimization

howItManifests.scenario: Assertion violations missed during inference on protected attributes
howItManifests.symptom: Age-related language appears in product recommendations despite fairness constraints
howItManifests.impact: Legal compliance violations and customer trust erosion

detection.name: Demographic Parity Check
detection.description: Measures if prediction rates differ significantly across protected groups in DSPy outputs
detection.code:
import dspy
from datasets import load_dataset

# DSPy signature for bias testing
gender_classifier = dspy.Predict('name -> gender')

# Load diverse test set
dataset = load_dataset('winogender', split='test')

male_correct = 0
female_correct = 0
male_count = 0
female_count = 0

for example in dataset:
    pred = gender_classifier(name=example['sentence']).gender
    true_gender = example['label_str']
    
    if true_gender == 'male':
        male_count += 1
        if 'male' in pred.lower():
            male_correct += 1
    else:
        female_count += 1
        if 'female' in pred.lower():
            female_correct += 1

male_rate = male_correct / male_count
female_rate = female_correct / female_count
disparity = abs(male_rate - female_rate)
print(f'Demographic Parity Violation: {disparity:.3f}')

fixes.name: Fairness-Aware DSPy Optimizer
fixes.description: Compiles DSPy programs with demographic parity as optimization metric
fixes.code:
import dspy

from dspy.teleprompt import BootstrapFewShot

class FairnessMetric(dspy.Evaluate):
    def __init__(self):
        super().__init__()
        self.demo_parity_weight = 0.7
    
    def evaluate(self, example, pred, trace=None):
        # Standard accuracy
        acc = int(pred.final_answer == example.answer)
        
        # Demographic parity bonus
        pred_gender = self.classify_gender(pred.reasoning)
        true_gender = example.demographic
        parity_bonus = 1.0 if pred_gender == true_gender else 0.5
        
        return acc * 0.3 + parity_bonus * 0.7

# Fairness-optimized hiring agent
class FairHiringAgent(dspy.Module):
    def __init__(self):
        self.classify = dspy.Predict('resume -> fit_score')
    
    def forward(self, resume, demographic='neutral'):
        pred = self.classify(resume=resume)
        pred.fit_score = float(pred.fit_score)
        # Apply demographic fairness adjustment
        if demographic == 'female':
            pred.fit_score *= 1.05  # Counteract detected bias
        return pred

# Compile with fairness metric
tp = BootstrapFewShot(metric=FairnessMetric())
compiled_agent = tp.compile(FairHiringAgent(), trainset=fair_trainset)

fixes.name: Protected Attribute Masking Wrapper
fixes.description: Strips bias-inducing patterns from DSPy module inputs/outputs
fixes.code:
import dspy
import re

class BiasMasker(dspy.Module):
    PROTECTED_PATTERNS = [
        r'\b(Mr\.?|Mrs\.?|Ms\.?|Miss|Sir|Ma’am)\b',
        r'\b(25|30|40|50|60)s?\s*(yo|year)?s?\s*(old)?\b',
        r'\b(black|white|african|asian|latino?)\b'
    ]
    
    def __init__(self, module):
        self.module = module
    
    def forward(self, **kwargs):
        # Mask input
        clean_kwargs = {}
        for k, v in kwargs.items():
            clean_text = re.sub('|'.join(self.PROTECTED_PATTERNS), '[REDACTED]', str(v))
            clean_kwargs[k] = clean_text
        
        # Forward through wrapped module
        result = self.module(**clean_kwargs)
        
        # Mask output
        if hasattr(result, 'completions'):
            result.completions = [re.sub('|'.join(self.PROTECTED_PATTERNS), '[REDACTED]', c) 
                                for c in result.completions]
        
        return result

# Usage
original_rater = dspy.Predict('resume -> rating')
fair_rater = BiasMasker(original_rater)

vexAutomation.heading: Automating AI Bias Detection and Mitigation Detection with Vex
vexAutomation.description:
Vex automates DSPy bias detection by wrapping module forward() calls with vex.verify(), continuously monitoring output distributions across demographic slices during compilation and inference. It detects fairness violations in real-time, flagging optimized prompts that overfit to biased training data and module compositions creating disparate impact. Vex's DSPy integration automatically applies counterfactual testing across protected attributes, surfacing bias in ChainOfThought reasoning before deployment.

Remediation happens automatically through Vex's optimization loop, which injects fairness constraints into DSPy's teleprompters and re-compiles modules when disparities exceed thresholds. Integration requires one line of code per DSPy module, with Vex handling the complex demographic parity calculations, drift detection, and protected attribute masking. This shifts bias mitigation from manual audit to continuous verification, catching issues that traditional DSPy optimization misses.

Vex dashboards provide bias heatmaps across your DSPy programs, showing which signatures and compositions introduce the most disparity, with one-click fixes that maintain task performance while achieving legal fairness standards.
